title: mentha pulegium var. exigua
intro: Mentha pulegium var. exigua, also known as small pennyroyal, is a subspecies of the pennyroyal plant, a low-growing perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of menthone, a compound with antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing digestive issues, alleviating menstrual cramps, and exhibiting antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it useful for topical applications and herbal remedies. Small pennyroyal can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, essential oils, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
